page: Amazon Web Services c6gn.16xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by the 64-core AWS Graviton2 processor using the 64-bit ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 architecture. Operating at 2.5 GHz, this server features 64 dedicated physical cores with a single thread per core, backed by 128.0 GB of system memory, yielding a 2.0 GB per core ratio. The CPU cache configuration includes 64 KB of L1 data cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache per core, and a shared 32 MB L3 cache. It operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor and does not include local NVMe storage or GPU accelerators.

A key feature of the c6gn.16xlarge is its high-speed networking, delivering a baseline network bandwidth of 100 Gbps. Benchmark results demonstrate strong multi-core performance, with stress-ng multi-core metrics and software compilation tasks placing in the top 25% of tested systems. Cached memory bandwidth read-only operations are exceptionally high at 3782.81 GB/sec. However, single-core performance is more moderate, as evidenced by average single-core stress-ng results and weaker single-threaded compression and text processing benchmarks.

Given its dedicated ARM64 architecture and high network throughput, this instance is highly cost-efficient for scale-out, network-bound workloads that benefit from physical core isolation without hyperthreading. It is particularly well-suited for high-throughput static web serving, Redis in-memory databases, and multi-threaded software compilation. Conversely, it is less optimal for single-threaded tasks or large-scale LLM inference, where its text generation speeds fall into lower performance tiers.
description: Amazon Web Services c6gn.16xlarge is a compute-optimized, network-optimized instance powered by a 64-core AWS Graviton2 ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 processor running at 2.5 GHz. Built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, it features 64 dedicated physical cores with one thread per core, 128.0 GB of system memory, and a baseline network bandwidth of 100 Gbps, with no local storage or GPU accelerators. The instance exhibits strong multi-core capabilities, particularly in software compilation, Redis operations, and static web serving, where it achieves high throughput. However, single-core performance and text processing benchmarks are relatively weak. This hardware profile offers high resource density and qualitative cost efficiency for scale-out, network-intensive applications. It is best suited for high-throughput web serving, distributed data stores, and multi-threaded containerized workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c6gn.16xlarge is a compute-optimized ARM64 server featuring 64 dedicated Graviton2 cores, 128 GB RAM, and 100 Gbps network bandwidth, ideal for high-throughput network and multi-threaded workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c6gn.16xlarge is an ARM64 compute-optimized instance with 64 dedicated cores, 128 GB RAM, and 100 Gbps baseline network bandwidth.
tagline: A high-bandwidth, 64-core ARM64 instance optimized for network-intensive workloads, multi-threaded applications, and high-throughput web serving.
bullet_points: Powered by a 64-core AWS Graviton2 ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 processor at 2.5 GHz
Features 128 GB of RAM and a baseline network bandwidth of 100 Gbps
Strong multi-core performance in software compilation and Redis operations
Dedicated physical cores with no hyperthreading for predictable execution
Best suited for static web serving, distributed caching, and containerized microservices
